characters.name: Alex Mercer
characters.description: Alex MercerBiographical informationFull NameAlex MercerNickname(s)Hotdog(byWillie)GenderMaleDate of birth1977-1978Date of deathJuly 22, 1995Birth placeLos Angeles, CaliforniaCause of deathFood PoisoningEye colorGreenHair colorBlondeSpeciesHuman (formerly)Ghost (currently)StatusDeceasedPersonal InformationEducationHigh School DropoutOccupationMusicianSingerAffilationsSunset Curve (formerly)Julie and the Phantoms (currently)FamilyUnnamed motherUnnamed fatherUnnamed older sisterFriendsLuke PattersonReggie PetersJulie MolinaWillie(mutual crush)FlynnTrevor Wilson(formerly)RomancesWillie(mutual crush)EnemiesCaleb CovingtonTrevor WilsonApperanceFirst episodeWake UpLast episodeStand TallPortrayed byOwen Patrick Joyner Ghost (currently) Singer Alex is a very anxious person with a tendency to overthink and worry about the details, more so than the other two phantoms. He started playing drums to help him relax due to his anxiety. He has a tendency to be modest and dismissive about his abilities (drumming, dancing, etc.), but easily acknowledges his better traits with the gentle encouragement of others, particularly his friends. Alex is very kind hearted and empathetic, as shown through his concern for Julie and his easy forgiveness of Willie's actions after processing his predicament with Caleb. At the same time, Alex can be very sarcastic. His quips are often in retort to his friends or to the situation he finds himself in. Of the Sunset Curve trio, he seems to have the most level-headed personality, although he also has his moments of drama and leaps of logic. He can also be very silly, as evidenced by his improvised dance during Dirty Candy's performance ofAll Eyes on Me.

characters.name: Caleb Covington
characters.description: Caleb CovingtonBiographical informationFull NameCaleb CovingtonGenderMaleDate of deathc. 1920sCause of deathPerforming a magic trickEye colorLight BlueHair colorDark BrownSpeciesGhost (currently)Human (formerly)StatusDeceasedPersonal InformationOccupationMagicianOwner and entertainer of the Hollywood Ghost ClubEnemiesLuke PattersonAlex MercerReggie PetersApperanceFirst episodeWake UpLast episodeStand TallPortrayed byCheyenne Jackson Owner and entertainer of the Hollywood Ghost Club Caleb Covingtonis the overall main antagonist inJulie and the Phantoms. He is the owner ofHollywood Ghost Club. Caleb Covington is a very manipulative individual. A natural showman, Caleb has the flair of the dramatic and theatrics within the Hollywood Ghost Club, probably stemming from his background as a famous magician. Moreover, Caleb is shown to be power-hungry and controlling, as he is shown to be obsessed with incorporating Luke, Reggie, and Alex in his house band and under his control because of their ability to be seen by lifers when performing, as well as his behavior towards Willie, one of the ghosts under his control. His willingness to force the boys into doing what he wants reveals his immoral character.

characters.name: Carlos Molina
characters.description: Carlos MolinaBiographical informationFull NameCarlos MolinaGenderMaleDate of birthLate 2000sBirth placeLos Angeles, CaliforniaEye colorDark BrownHair colorBrownSpeciesHumanStatusAlivePersonal InformationOccupationStudentAthleteFamilyRay Molina(father)Rose Molina† (mother)Julie Molina(older sister)Victoria(maternal aunt)FriendsFlynn(friend)ApperanceFirst episodeWake UpLast episodeStand TallPortrayed bySonny Bustamante Rose Molina† (mother)Julie Molina(older sister)Victoria(maternal aunt) Carlos Molinais a recurring characterJulie and the Phantoms. He is the son ofRoseandRay Molina. He isJulie'syounger brother. Carlos, for all intents and purposes, has an ordinary younger brother personality. His care for Julie and his family is demonstrated in his small actions, trying to distract Ray when Julie is in trouble, and setting out to hunt and 'protect' her from ghosts. Carlos believes in the supernatural—particularly in ghosts—and he spends most of the season one testing out ghost theories from YouTube videos. His overactive imagination combined with the phantoms' actual presence in the Molinas' house lead him further in his belief in the existence of ghosts.

characters.name: Carrie Wilson
characters.description: Carrie WilsonBiographical informationFull NameCarrie WilsonGenderFemaleDate of birth2004-2005 (age 15-16)Birth placeLos Angeles, CaliforniaEye colorBrownHair colorBrownPastel Pink (wig for performances)SpeciesHumanStatusAlivePersonal InformationEducationLos Feliz High SchoolOccupationStudentDancerSingerFamilyTrevor Wilson(father)Unnamed MotherUnnamed Paternal UnclesUnnamed Paternal GrandfatherUnnamed Paternal GrandmotherFriendsKayla(best friend)Three Unnamed Members ofDirty CandyRomancesNick(ex-boyfriend)EnemiesJulie Molina(former childhood best friend/rival)Flynn(former friend/rival)ApperanceFirst episodeWake UpLast episodeStand TallPortrayed bySavannah Lee May Pastel Pink (wig for performances) Singer Carrie comes off as a mean spirited person towards Julie and Flynn. She is the most popular girl at school with her musical groupDirty Candy. She gets very jealous of the attentionJulie and the Phantomsget, believing her group to be better. She is ambitious and a self-proclaimed hard worker. InBright, she told Nick that she worked hard onDirty Candyroutines, and believed they were deserving of more recognition.

characters.name: Emily Patterson
characters.description: Emily PattersonBiographical informationFull NameEmily PattersonGenderFemaleSpeciesHumanStatusAlivePersonal InformationFamilyMitch Patterson(husband)Luke Patterson† (son)ApperanceFirst episodeFinally FreeLast episodeUnsaid EmilyPortrayed byJennifer-Juniper Angeli Luke Patterson† (son) EmilyPattersonisLuke Patterson's mother and the subject of the song he wrote titledUnsaid Emily. She is played byJennifer-Juniper Angeli. Not much is known about Emily's personality, although in the songUnsaid Emily, Luke notes that "we're alike that way" in their stubbornness.

characters.name: Flynn Taylor
characters.description: FlynnBiographical informationFull NameFlynn TaylorNickname(s)Disappointment(by Julie)GenderFemaleDate of birth2004-2005 (15 years old)Birth placeLos Angeles, CaliforniaEye colorBrownHair colorBlackSpeciesHumanStatusAlivePersonal InformationEducationLos Feliz High SchoolOccupationStudentDJ/RapperAffilationsDouble TroubleJulie and the Phantoms(band manager)FamilyUnnamed fatherMisha Taylor (mother)FriendsJulie(best friend)Carlos MolinaLuke PattersonAlex MercerReggie PetersNickEnemiesCarrie Wilson(former friend)ApperanceFirst episodeWake UpLast episodeStand TallPortrayed byJadah Marie Misha Taylor (mother) Flynn Tayloris a deuteragonist inJulie and the Phantoms. She isJulie Molina's best friend and is one of the few people who knows the truth about the Phantoms. Flynn is grounded, loyal, and a very assertive person, who values honesty and her friendship with Julie. She was fully skeptical of the idea of ghosts before she saw them with her own eyes. While she grew to enjoy the idea and appearance of the three boys, as well as the music they made with Julie, Flynn is consistently focused on the fact that they are dead and are intangible, unlike Julie who has the benefit of seeing and regularly interacting with them. Her loyalty is apparent in her support of Julie through her mother's death, as well as the moments when Carrie tries to dismiss Julie. This loyalty and easy-going behavior only wavers when she feels she is treated badly, as seen inFlying Solowhen she attempts to egg Julie's house to keep secrets from her. It also leads her to be intolerant of bullies. This is shown through her hostility and dislike towards Carrie, more so than even Julie, who is often a target of Carrie's insults. Flynn was always pushing Julie to not be shy, and tell Luke how she truly felt about him. Flynn's self-confidence leads her to take initiative, oftentimes applying her creativity and hard work (signing up Julie's band for the dance without permission, handling the band's social media, organizing a garage party for their Edge of Great concert, etc.).

characters.name: Julie Molina
characters.description: Julie MolinaBiographical informationFull NameJulie MolinaNickname(s)Jules (byFlynn)Underachiever (byFlynn)Mija (byRay,Victoria)GenderFemaleDate of birth2004-2005 (15 years old)Birth placeLos Angeles, CaliforniaEye colorBrownHair colorBrownSpeciesHumanStatusAlivePersonal InformationEducationLos Feliz High SchoolOccupationStudentMusicianSingerSongwriterFamilyRay Molina(father)Rose Molina† (mother)Carlos Molina(younger brother)Victoria(maternal aunt)FriendsFlynn Taylor(best friend)Luke Patterson(good friend, bandmate, mutual crush)Reggie Peters(good friend, bandmate)Alex Mercer(good friend, bandmate)Nick(friend, former crush)RomancesLuke Patterson(mutual crush)Nick(former crush)EnemiesCarrie Wilson(former best friend)Caleb CovingtonApperanceFirst episodeWake UpLast episodeStand TallPortrayed byMadison Reyes Underachiever (byFlynn)Mija (byRay,Victoria) SingerSongwriter Julie is headstrong, determined, loyal, caring, loving, and has an amazing voice. As said byReggie,she “has a voice of an angel”. She values friendship, her family, and music over everything else. She's not afraid to stand up for herself and speak her mind when she gets annoyed. She can be nervous or sometimes shy to people but her confidence rises when she’s singing or playing music. She's very creative, talented and artistic. In the first season, Julie carries a deep grief from the death of her mother, which has affected her ability to perform music. This grief is present even past the first episode, when she decides to play music again. It influences how Julie perceives her interactions with other people. Julie is committed to what she deems matters. She tries to keep her promises, which include attending and doing well in school as a promise to her Dad, telling Flynn about ghosts because she doesn't want to keep a secret, not ditching school to be a dance partner for Nick, and feeling hurt when the phantoms bailed on her at the school dance. However, Julie also has shown no trouble with breaking the rules once in a while. In the instances when she doesn't do things by the book, she is usually motivated by two things: a need to mitigate the chaos caused by the phantoms (climbing over Carrie's fence to stop the phantoms' pranking, replacing the opening band so the trio can perform at the Orpheum) or for her own music (performing Bright at the assembly without permission, sneaking out to perform Finally Free).

characters.name: Kayla
characters.description: KaylaBiographical informationFull NameKaylaGenderFemaleDate of birth2004-2005 (age 15-16)Birth placeUnited StatesEye colorBrownHair colorBlack (natural)Purple (wig for performing)SpeciesHumanStatusAlivePersonal InformationEducationLos Feliz High SchoolOccupationStudentDancerSingerAffilationsDirty CandyFriendsCarrie Wilson(Bandmate/Best Friend)Three Unnamed Members ofDirty Candy(Bandmates/Friends)ApperanceFirst episodeBrightLast episodeFinally FreePortrayed byVictoria Caro Three Unnamed Members ofDirty Candy(Bandmates/Friends) Kaylais a recurring character in the Netflix original seriesJulie and the Phantoms. She is a part ofDirty Candy,Carrie Wilson's girl dance group. She isCarrie Wilson's Best Friend. She is portrayed byVictoria Caro. Not much is known about her personality but she seems to be smart or at least smart enough to know what Holograms are when she asksJulieifAlex,Luke, andReggieare Holograms. She is a very talented dancer.

characters.name: Kayla

characters.name: Luke Patterson
characters.description: Luke PattersonBiographical informationFull NameLuke PattersonGenderMaleDate of birth1977Date of deathJuly 22, 1995Birth placeLos Angeles, CaliforniaCause of deathFood PoisoningEye colorHazelHair colorBrownSpeciesHuman(formerly)Ghost(currently)StatusDeceasedPersonal InformationEducationDeath Before High School GraduationOccupationMusicianComposerSingerDiner Employee (formerly)FamilyEmily Patterson(mother)Mitch Patterson(father)FriendsAlex MercerReggie PetersJulie Molina(crush)FlynnWillieTrevor Wilson(formerly)RomancesJulie Molina(mutual crush)EnemiesCaleb CovingtonTrevor WilsonApperanceFirst episodeWake UpLast episodeStand TallPortrayed byCharlie Gillespie Ghost(currently) Mitch Patterson(father) Luke has a very upbeat and fast-paced personality. He is easy-going about everything except his music— often emphasizing the importance of it to his friends in conversation. Luke prioritizes this before anything else. His passion for music can sometimes have negative consequences, however. He reacted with anger that Bobby/Trevor Wilson stole his songs, more so than his other two bandmates who, while irked at the lack of mention, were more focused on the material wealth Bobby had accrued. This eventually causes him to abandon rehearsal to prank Bobby at his mansion and to forget Julie at her school dance. He ran away from home for his music and it's implied inEdge of Greatthat he frequently skipped classes to go rehearse. His lack of priorities can get mistaken for selfishness (see Julie inFinally Free), although those close to him knows that Luke genuinely believes his music can connect him to people on another, deeper level. On equal level to his music are his friends. He is close with Reggie and Alex and thinks of them as family—this is later applied to Julie as well. It's notable that the people he is closest with are those he connects to through their shared music.

characters.name: Nick
characters.description: NickBiographical informationFull NameNickNickname(s)Nicky PooGenderMaleDate of birth2004/2005 (15/16-year-old)Birth placeLos Angeles, CaliforniaEye colorBlueHair colorBlondeSpeciesHumanStatusAlivePersonal InformationEducationLos Feliz High SchoolOccupationStudentAthleteMusicianSingerFamilyUnnamed younger sisterFriendsJulie Molina(crush/friend)Flynn TaylorLoz Feliz Lacrosse TeamRomancesCarrie Wilson(ex-girlfriend)Julie Molina(crush)ApperanceFirst episodeWake UpLast episodeStand TallPortrayed bySacha Carlson Loz Feliz Lacrosse Team Julie Molina(crush) Nick is known as "a sweetheart" to Julie, even though she hasn't had a real conversation with him according to Flynn (Wake Up). This statement can be relied upon as Nick is shown to be popular in school and well-liked by his classmates and friends. In numerous instances in season one, Nick is shown to be friendly to Julie and Flynn despite Carrie's dislike of them, and even moves to break up with Carrie when he feels her hostility has grown too much.

characters.name: Principal Lessa
characters.description: Principal LessaBiographical informationGenderFemaleSpeciesHumanStatusAlivePersonal InformationOccupationPrincipal ofLos Feliz High SchoolApperanceFirst episodeBrightLast episodeFlying SoloPortrayed byPrincipal Lessais the principal of Los Feliz High School, a performing school in California. After Julie performsBrightwith her "hologram band," Principal Lessa offers to let Julie stay at Los Feliz, saying she expects to be thanked when Julie wins her first Grammy(Flying Solo) Principal Lessa is only featured briefly in the series, so not much is known about her personality. However, from her brief interaction with Julie, she appears to be both kind and good-humored. Principal Lessa is only featured briefly in the series, so not much is known about her personality. However, from her brief interaction with Julie, she appears to be both kind and good-humored.

characters.name: Ray Molina
characters.description: Ray MolinaBiographical informationFull NameRay MolinaNickname(s)Dad (byJulieandCarlos)GenderMaleEye colorBlueHair colorGreySpeciesHumanStatusAlivePersonal InformationOccupationPhotographer (presumably)FamilyRose Molina† (wife)Julie Molina(daughter)Carlos Molina(son)Victoria(sister-in-law)FriendsReggie (one-sided, on Reggie's side)ApperanceFirst episodeWake UpLast episodeStand TallPortrayed byCarlos Ponce Julie Molina(daughter)Carlos Molina(son)Victoria(sister-in-law) Ray Molinais a recurring character inJulie and the Phantoms. He is the father ofJulieandCarlos. He is the husband to the deceasedRoseand brother-in-law toVictoria. Ray is a very kind and supportive father, as shown when he was willing to enroll Julie in a school in Australia after she was temporarily kicked out of her school's music program so she could continue her budding career in music. He is also very patient and understanding with his children, but knows how to lay down the law in a way that isn't too overbearing. He is skeptical of the existence of the supernatural, but is willing to give the idea a chance, even if only to humour his son. Due to his parenting style being more laid-back and trusting, he has a tendency to draw the ire of his sister-in-law, Victoria, who believes in a much firmer approach to raising children. However, the two get along well and are quite close. He deeply misses his wife, and sometimes talks to her as if she were still alive. Ray always takes his children's opinions into consideration, acknowledging their intelligence and independence. He is deeply thankful to the Phantoms for bringing his daughter back to life and wishes to meet them, though he is understanding that the situation is difficult, believing they live far away.

characters.name: Reggie Peters
characters.description: Reggie PetersBiographical informationFull NameReginald PetersNickname(s)Reggie(by everyone)Reg(by Alex)GenderMaleDate of birth1977-1978Date of deathJuly 22, 1995 (age 17)Birth placeLos Angeles, CaliforniaCause of deathFood PoisoningEye colorGreenHair colorDark BrownSpeciesHuman (formerly)Ghost (currently)StatusDeceasedPersonal InformationOccupationMusicianSingerFamilyUnknown motherUnknown fatherSteve Peters (little brother)FriendsLuke PattersonAlex MercerJulie MolinaFlynnWillieRay Molina(one-sided)Trevor Wilson(formerly)EnemiesCaleb CovingtonApperanceFirst episodeWake UpLast episodeStand TallPortrayed byJeremy Shada Ghost (currently) Singer Reggie is very upbeat, bubbly and almost always happy. He can be a bit of an airhead at times though the group still loves him. Reggie finds great comfort in his friends, and very likely finds his happiness through spending time with them He is concerned that about the possibility of no longer being with Alex and Luke once they cross over. Reggie is also always ready to support and give compliments to his friends, such as praising Alex's drumming to Julie's singing. He is a bit of a flirt. This is demonstrated when he flirts with Rose, flirts with the girls in front of the Orpheum in 1995, winks atKaylaacross the room while he's performingBright, wondering if the cheerleaders at Julie's school noticed him. He also immediately notices when someone thinks he's cute. He never misses a chance to pay a compliment, call someone cute, beautiful, etc.

characters.name: Trevor Wilson
characters.description: Trevor Wilson20201995Biographical informationFull NameBobby Shaw (formerly)Trevor WilsonGenderMaleDate of birthMarch 16, 1978Birth placeLos Feliz, CaliforniaEye colorBrownHair colorDark BrownSpeciesHumanStatusAlivePersonal InformationOccupationMusicianFamilyUnnamed fatherUnnamed motherUnnamed older brothersCarrie Wilson(daughter)FriendsRose Molina(friend)RomancesCarrie Wilson's mother (partner or ex-partner)EnemiesLuke(former friend/bandmate)Alex(former friend/bandmate)Reggie(former friend/bandmate)ApperanceFirst episodeWake Up(young)I Got the Music(adult)Last episodeUnsaid Emily(young)Stand Tall(adult)Portrayed byTaylor Kare(young)Steve Bacic(adult) Trevor Wilson Carrie Wilson(daughter) Young Bobby as a part of Sunset Curve is not seen to have an exact personality. He is the first to speak after they practiceNow or Never, encouraging the boys saying that they had played well. He is shown playing with his bandmates in the garage duringUnsaid Emily, meaning he was close enough to witness a vulnerable moment. As Trevor, we see that he seems like your typical rockstar. He wears shades in doors, and a leather jacket. Trevor is shown to frequently meditate, and goes to therapy. (I Got the Music) Trevor seems to be supportive and affectionate with his daughter Carrie, exemplified by his willingness to pay forDirty Candyexpenses and his declaration of "only love" to her inStand Tall.

characters.name: Victoria
characters.description: VictoriaBiographical informationFull NameVictoriaGenderFemaleSpeciesHumanStatusAlivePersonal InformationFamilyRose Molina† (sister)Ray Molina(brother-in-law)Julie Molina(niece)Carlos Molina(nephew)ApperanceFirst episodeWake UpLast episodeStand TallPortrayed byAlison ArayaTia Victoriais a recurring character in the Netflix seriesJulie and the Phantoms, and is the aunt toJulieandCarlos, and the sister-in-law toRay. Ray Molina(brother-in-law)Julie Molina(niece)Carlos Molina(nephew) She is portrayed byAlison Araya. Victoria is pragmatic and straitlaced individual, and cares deeply about her family. After Rose died, she began checking up on the Molina's and bringing meals for them to eat for dinner. Sometimes, Victoria's practicality and set way of doing things gets in the way of emotionally connecting with her family despite the obvious familial love between all of them. At the start of season one, she is vocal about her encouragement for the Molinas to move out of their house, despite Ray's stifled reluctance, and about her encouragement of Julie dropping out of the music program, despite Julie's previous passion for it. The Molinas feel they have to hide how slowly they are processing Rose's death when she is around. It is obvious, however, from Ray's vocal appreciation about her help parenting Julie and Carlos, to her FaceTime calls with Carlos and her presence during Julie's performance at the Orpheum, that she is well-loved and appreciated.

It is obvious, however, from Ray's vocal appreciation about her help parenting Julie and Carlos, to her FaceTime calls with Carlos and her presence during Julie's performance at the Orpheum, that she is well-loved and appreciated.
characters.personality_section: Victoria is pragmatic and straitlaced individual, and cares deeply about her family. After Rose died, she began checking up on the Molina's and bringing meals for them to eat for dinner. Sometimes, Victoria's practicality and set way of doing things gets in the way of emotionally connecting with her family despite the obvious familial love between all of them. At the start of season one, she is vocal about her encouragement for the Molinas to move out of their house, despite Ray's stifled reluctance, and about her encouragement of Julie dropping out of the music program, despite Julie's previous passion for it. The Molinas feel they have to hide how slowly they are processing Rose's death when she is around. It is obvious, however, from Ray's vocal appreciation about her help parenting Julie and Carlos, to her FaceTime calls with Carlos and her presence during Julie's performance at the Orpheum, that she is well-loved and appreciated.

characters.name: Willie
characters.description: WillieBiographical informationFull NameWilliamNickname(s)WillieGenderMaleCause of deathHit by a car while skateboardingEye colorBrownHair colorDark BrownSpeciesHuman (formerly)Ghost (currently)StatusDeceasedPersonal InformationOccupationSkateboarderMember of theHollywood Ghost ClubFriendsAlex Mercer(friend/crush)Luke Patterson(friend)Reggie Peters(friend)RomancesAlex Mercer(mutual crush)EnemiesCaleb CovingtonCopsSandApperanceFirst episodeFlying SoloLast episodeStand TallPortrayed byBooboo Stewart Ghost (currently) Luke Patterson(friend)Reggie Peters(friend) Willie is vibrant and friendly. He’s energetic, and jumps at the chance to help out a person in need, especially if it involves and form of rule-breaking. Willie doesn't wait for a reason to the rules, however; skating in Justin Bieber's empty pool and breaking into a closed museum to skate with Alex are a few examples of this. Along with his regard for spontaneity, he's seen to be courteous and aware of other's feelings. He offers a listening ear to Alex in distress, and offers methods of emotional regulation. When trouble arises and Willie finds out he has unwittingly put Alex, Reggie, and Luke at risk, he's quick to amend this, Only prioritizing himself for a short time before going as far to put his whole afterlife at risk.
